what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that links an attachment identifier to a board item target in the dragnet-development channel, declaring it as a board_items type and recording when it was added.
observations: The file contains only YAML front matter with no body content.
target uses a board:// URI pointing to item-02d8b53b.
standouts: It is a cross-reference artifact between a channel attachment and a board item via a board:// URI.
It carries no payload beyond identity, type, target, and provenance timestamp.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
References external entities with a board:// URI scheme.
File name matches the declared attachment id.
Body after front matter is empty.
concerns: No description or payload content is provided, so the attachment's semantic purpose is not self-contained.
The referenced board item is not present in this file.
notes: A provenance stub that records a board-item attachment in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or discussion does this attachment capture?
What is the nature of board://item-02d8b53b?
Is the attachment automatically generated or manually curated?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an attachment index entry; it matches that purpose, though it contains no content payload.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to create a durable, queryable record of a board item attached to a channel conversation, enabling later retrieval by attachment id.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI as the target for a channel attachment is a distinct linking pattern.
contribution: It contributes a precise attachment-to-board-item reference with timestamped provenance, which a fused system would keep to maintain the channel-board relationship.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file that maps attachment att-d077fb3c in the dragnet-development channel to board item item-02d8b53b as a board_items type with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record and make discoverable the association between a channel attachment and a specific board item.
